title: Soliton resolution along a sequence of times for the energy critical wave equation
speaker: Carlos Kenig
abstract: Analysis and Beyond - Celebrating Jean Bourgain's Work and Impact

proof_stages.label: Global-dynamics framing and critical model
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates soliton resolution as a solitons-plus-radiation asymptotic problem and sets up the focusing energy-critical wave equation, its scaling, conserved energy, and defocusing scattering contrast.

proof_stages.label: Bounded regime and soliton candidates
proof_stages.summary: Blow-up and non-scattering phenomena motivate restricting soliton resolution to bounded energy-norm dynamics, while elliptic stationary states, Lorentz-transformed traveling waves, and the finite singular set define the nonlinear objects and local geometry to be resolved.

proof_stages.label: Radial channels of energy
proof_stages.summary: In the radial three-dimensional setting, a linear outer-energy inequality yields a dynamical characterization of the ground state, and after extracting the regular part a profile decomposition plus finite speed of propagation forces every nonradiative block to be a copy of the ground state.

proof_stages.label: Virial subsequence mechanism and radial extensions
proof_stages.summary: Pohozaev-type and virial identities, together with absence of self-similar blow-up, give small time derivative along well-chosen sequences and allow radial soliton-resolution decompositions in dimensions where direct channel estimates are unavailable or insufficient.

proof_stages.label: Nonradial formulation, radiation, and theorem
proof_stages.summary: Because nonradial traveling waves are unclassified and generic outer-energy inequalities fail, the theorem is formulated as local-in-space soliton resolution along selected times after extracting a finite-time regular part or infinite-time scattering profile.

proof_stages.label: Cone localization and flux/Morawetz control
proof_stages.summary: Near a chosen singular cone, coincidence with the regular part gives boundary L6 control and hence controllable flux, which enables a wave-map-inspired Morawetz estimate producing a sublogarithmic spacetime bound that vanishes in the relevant limit.

proof_stages.label: Profile classification and final error removal
proof_stages.summary: A Tauberian upgrade of the vanishing quantity classifies nonlinear profiles by ruling out self-similar blocks and identifying small-scale blocks as traveling waves, after which a tailored outgoing channel argument eliminates the remaining dispersive error.
